title: Response Status, Success Variants, Failure Taxonomy, and Problem Details · Automation and Data Client Systems · Program Forensics
summary: A data reliability engineer tracing a failed overnight import must isolate a plausible but incorrect result from conflicting traces, repair its cause, and retain a regression witness for response status, success variants, failure taxonomy, and problem details, producing typed response and failure classifier. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers typed response and failure classifier and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regressio…
objectives: Interpret informational, successful, redirection, client-error, and server-error status codes as protocol metadata. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Handle 201, 202, 204, 206, and other success responses with their location, body, and completion implications. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Separate request construction, transport, status, representation, and domain failures in a stable client contract. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Represent partial results and unknown write outcomes so callers do not silently retry or declare success. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

hints: For Python Publish the incident record · Debug Partial And Ambiguous Outcomes, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Partial And Ambiguous Outcomes precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Python Publish the incident record · Debug Partial And Ambiguous Outcomes, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: overnight ingestion repair client observes 202 without a result, 204 with no content, 206 with a range, 429 with Retry-After, and 503 problem+json for case P-2. Preserve status, transport, schema, domain, partial, and unknown-effect outcomes separately. Competency probe: Represent partial results and unknown write outcomes so callers do not silently retry or declare success. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

For Python Publish the incident record · Debug Partial And Ambiguous Outcomes, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: A timeout proves the server did not perform the requested operation. This remains false for Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers.
